items.headline: Water utility launches financial assistance pilot program
items.summary: San José is rolling out a financial assistance pilot program for water customers, likely aimed at helping residents manage utility costs during drought or affordability challenges. This addresses growing water bill concerns in the region.

items.headline: 27-unit townhome project proposed with affordable units
items.summary: The Planning Commission considered a residential development featuring 27 three-story townhomes, including 5 affordable units. This represents a moderate-density housing addition to Cupertino's residential stock.

items.headline: City Council to vote on 72-unit affordable housing at Lytton Avenue
items.summary: The council will hold a public hearing on a six-story, 100% affordable housing project with 72 units planned for 450 Lytton Avenue, replacing a city-owned surface parking lot. The project is seeking density bonus concessions under state law to fit 72 units on a small 0.43-acre parcel.

items.headline: Power upgrade permitted for R&D expansion at Porter Drive
items.summary: Palo Alto has cleared electrical infrastructure improvements at 3210 Porter Drive to support new research and development work at the existing building. The project includes new transformers and conduit extending to a substation on Hanover Drive, signaling continued tech/R&D activity in the area.

items.headline: New single-family home permitted in San José under SB9 law
items.summary: A new detached home at 326 Battle Dance Dr receives a building permit, representing one of the few traditional single-family infill projects in the city. This SB9 project is notable as state law allows property owners to split lots and build additional homes on single-family parcels, a tool rarely utilized in San José.

items.headline: Campbell adopts policy restricting federal civil enforcement on city property
items.summary: The City Council passed a resolution prohibiting use of city-owned and operated property for federal civil enforcement actions. This policy limits federal agencies' ability to conduct certain operations on Campbell property.

items.headline: City declares overgrown brush and vegetation a public nuisance
items.summary: Los Gatos adopted a resolution formally classifying hazardous vegetation and brush as a public nuisance, enabling the city to take enforcement action against property owners who fail to maintain their land. This gives the city legal grounds to address fire hazards and overgrowth that pose risks to neighboring properties and public safety.

items.headline: City council approves digital signage agreement with Clear Channel
items.summary: San José amended its contract with Clear Channel Outdoor LLC for large format digital signage on city-owned property, potentially expanding advertising revenue or modifying signage terms across public spaces.
